strengths: Nearly two decades of hands-on full-stack engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems in multiple languages
Real low-latency distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, real-time data)
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and tooling for agentic workflows
concerns: No executive or people-leadership history: every role is an individual-contributor position (Senior/Staff), with zero evidence of scaling a team from 10 to 30+, building a leadership layer, governing outsourced partners, or reporting to a board

No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems — the agentic exposure is recent, tooling-adjacent contribution work, not designing, operating, or evaluating stateful agents with financial consequences
Thin regulated-fintech depth: one ~15-month stint at RIDE Capital as a developer, with no credit-risk, working-capital, liquidity, or multi-jurisdiction compliance architecture on record

key_factors.factor: Absence of engineering-leadership and org-scaling track record
No production multi-agent AI experience in high-stakes environments

response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, technically credible senior engineer, but the role is an executive CTO position whose hard requirements — 5+ years of senior technical leadership, scaling teams from 10 to 30+, developing internal leadership, governing hybrid delivery models, and board-level communication — are entirely unevidenced in a career spent as an individual contributor. The agentic-AI requirement is similarly unmet: contributing to MCP tooling for a year is not the demanded production experience building and operating multi-agent systems under regulatory and financial stakes, and his fintech exposure is brief and non-architectural. On the criteria that define this role, he is a weak fit.
